title: Developer Experience (DX) Definition of Done — ggen v26.5.28
purpose: Specify what makes development frictionless across 7 critical dimensions. Completion checklist for feature-complete DX.

dimensions.name: Command Ergonomics
dimensions.description: Single, memorable entry point for all dev tasks via `just`.
dimensions.why_it_matters: Developers should not hunt for the right command. One tool, all tasks, predictable syntax reduces cognitive load and eliminates command-line thrashing.
success_criteria: All critical tasks callable via `just <task>`
No direct `cargo` or `cargo make` calls required
Consistent command naming (lowercase, hyphen-separated)
Command runs with sensible timeout defaults
Help output (`just --list`) shows all available tasks with one-liner descriptions

critical_commands.use_case: Fastest feedback loop — syntax errors, type mismatches
RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle; run after writing code
Ensure doc examples are correct and runnable
Catch logical bugs, style violations; run before pre-commit

critical_commands.purpose: Full gate: fmt → check → lint → test-unit (fail-fast)
critical_commands.use_case: Before `git commit`; ensures all checks pass

critical_commands.purpose: Performance SLO validation (startup, build, RDF processing)
Build HTML API docs from /// comments
critical_commands.use_case: Review auto-generated docs; test doc comments

critical_commands.purpose: Full μ₁-μ₅ pipeline with cryptographic receipt
critical_commands.use_case: Generate from RDF spec; emit artifacts + receipt
Verify test quality; catch weak assertions

examples: Developer workflow: `just pre-commit && git commit -m 'feat: ...'` (all checks pass before commit)
Fast iteration: `just check && just test-unit` (sub-15s feedback)
Production validation: `just check && just test && just lint && just slo-check` (definition of done)

dimensions.name: Error Messages
dimensions.description: Errors and warnings must guide developers to root cause and recovery path.
dimensions.why_it_matters: A cryptic error stops a developer cold. Clear, actionable messages let them diagnose and fix without external help. Reduces time-to-resolution from hours to minutes.
success_criteria: Error messages name the file and line number (not just line number)
Error messages include the rule/invariant violated (reference to CLAUDE.md or rules)
Error messages include a recovery path (e.g., 'Fix by adding X' or 'See docs/troubleshooting.md')
Error messages avoid jargon; explain what went wrong in developer terms
Compiler errors are augmented with context (e.g., 'This violates GGEN-TPL-001: template consumes variable not in SELECT')
Validation errors link to diagnostic codes (GGEN-* codes from CLAUDE.md)
Timeout errors suggest increasing SLO or debugging bottleneck (not just 'timed out')

diagnostic_codes.meaning: Template consumes {{ var }} that SELECT does not produce
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Add {{ var }} to SELECT clause or remove from template
Ensure all variables in output_file pattern are bound in SELECT

diagnostic_codes.meaning: output_file escapes the project root (Layer Violation)
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Use relative paths that resolve within project root

diagnostic_codes.meaning: {file = ...} binding points at a missing file
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Ensure file exists or remove the binding
Use explicit SELECT columns for better type checking
Add ORDER BY clause for deterministic output

examples.good:
error[E0308]: expected `String`, found `&str`
  → src/main.rs:42:15
   |
42 | let x: String = "hello";
   |                 ^^^^^^^ expected `String`, found `&str`
   |
   = help: try: `let x: String = "hello".to_string();`

error[GGEN-QUERY-002]: SELECT * disables provision checking
  → ggen.toml:15:5
   |
15 | SELECT * WHERE { ... }
   |          ^ consider explicit column list
   |
   = hint: use `SELECT ?var1 ?var2 ...` instead

error: command timed out (120s)
  → just test (full test suite)
   |
   = This may indicate:
   - First compile (deps cached?): `cargo clean && just test` to reset
   - Slow test: add `#[ignore]` and run with `--include-ignored`
   - Infinite loop: check test for logic error
   = See: docs/troubleshooting.md#timeout

dimensions.name: Workflow Efficiency
dimensions.description: Keyboard shortcuts, aliases, and automation that let developers stay in their editor.
dimensions.why_it_matters: Context switching (editor → terminal → editor) breaks flow. Fast shortcuts and background automation keep developers in the zone. A 2-second keyboard shortcut vs. a 10-second context switch = 5x speedup.
success_criteria: Common tasks bound to keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+Shift+B for build, Ctrl+Shift+T for test)
Aliases for common `just` commands in `~/.bashrc` or `.zshrc` (e.g., `jc` → `just check`)
Cargo watch integration: `cargo watch -x 'just test-unit'` (hot reload on file change)
`cargo make` never called directly; all workflows route through `just`
Git hooks enforce quality gates before commit (pre-commit hook runs `just pre-commit`)
Git hooks prevent force-push to main (pre-push validation)
OTEL tracing toggled via env var, not code changes (e.g., `RUST_LOG=trace,ggen_ai=trace`)
Fast re-iteration loop: edit → save → auto-test → feedback <2s

automation.benefit: CI doesn't fail for preventable reasons
Compiler errors visible as you type
Debug external calls without code changes

examples: Developer saves file → cargo watch triggers `just test-unit` → 3s feedback loop
Developer edits ggen.toml → rust-analyzer highlights GGEN-TPL-001 violation in real-time
Developer tries to commit with failing tests → pre-commit hook runs `just pre-commit` → commit blocked until tests pass

dimensions.name: IDE Integration
dimensions.description: LSP, debugging, and editor support that surfaces compiler knowledge without context switches.
dimensions.why_it_matters: The best error message is one that appears before you compile. LSP, live diagnostics, and smart debugging let developers fix issues in-editor without leaving the IDE.
success_criteria: rust-analyzer-lsp enabled in .claude/settings.json
Compiler errors visible in editor as red squiggles (not after compile)
Hover over symbol shows type signature, trait bounds, visibility (not from memory or docs)
`Go to Definition` jumps to actual definition, respecting re-exports
`Find All References` finds all usages across 15-crate workspace (semantic, not text search)
`Go to Implementation` finds all trait implementers (no blanket impl misses)
Code completion suggests symbols from all crates, respecting visibility
Call hierarchy shows callers and callees (not manual grep)
Rename refactoring updates all usages atomically
Debugging: breakpoints, step-over, step-into, variable inspection
Performance profiling: flame graphs, memory profiling integration

lsp_navigation_rules.why: Grep finds text matches; LSP understands semantics. In a 30-crate workspace with heavy trait usage, text search gives wrong answers.

lsp_navigation_rules.task: List all symbols in a .rs file
lsp_navigation_rules.why: LSP gives structured symbol tree; manual scanning is error-prone.

lsp_navigation_rules.task: Find where a type or function is defined
lsp_navigation_rules.why: LSP respects module scoping, re-exports, visibility; grep finds all text matches.
LSP eliminates false positives (strings, comments, different `foo` in different scopes).

lsp_navigation_rules.task: Find all types implementing a trait
lsp_navigation_rules.why: LSP finds blanket impls, generics, conditional impls that grep misses.

lsp_navigation_rules.task: Get type signature, trait bounds, visibility
lsp_navigation_rules.why: LSP shows resolved types, including generics and trait bounds.
LSP gives complete call graph; manual chain misses indirect calls.

lsp_navigation_rules.task: Navigate mod / use to source file
lsp_navigation_rules.why: LSP handles re-exports and path resolution.

editor_features_required.implementation: rust-analyzer reports errors as you type
editor_features_required.benefit: Fix compiler errors before running cargo check

editor_features_required.implementation: rust-analyzer shows inferred types on hover
editor_features_required.benefit: Understand type signatures without reading impl
Discover APIs without manual docs lookup
Understand runtime behavior without print debugging

examples: Developer hovers over `PackRegistry` → sees full type signature, trait bounds, visibility
Developer clicks 'Go to Definition' on `load_packs_legacy()` → jumps to actual definition (not stub)
Developer right-clicks on trait name → 'Find Implementations' → sees all 12 implementers
Developer renames `SyncOptions` field → all 47 usages updated atomically

dimensions.name: Documentation Surface
dimensions.description: Quick references and in-editor help that answer 'how do I...' without leaving the IDE.
dimensions.why_it_matters: Documentation that's not in-editor is documentation that won't be read. A 5-second lookup in CLAUDE.md beats a 5-minute web search. Developers should never context-switch to find how to do something basic.
success_criteria: CLAUDE.md pinned in editor (single source of truth for rules, commands, workflow)
LSP docstring examples compile and run (`just test-doc` validates all examples)
Error messages link to relevant docs (e.g., 'See docs/troubleshooting.md#timeout')
`just --list` shows one-liner for each command (inline help, no docs lookup needed)
.claude/rules/ directory organized by topic (rust/, andon/, otel-validation.md, coding-agent-mistakes.md)
Rules auto-load based on front-matter (priority, category, auto_load flags)
Architecture reference updated (COMPRESSED_REFERENCE.md, LSP-surveyed crate map)
Troubleshooting guide covers common errors (timeout, flaky tests, build failures)
Quick-start guide for new developers (<5 min to first `just test-unit`)

documentation_map.purpose: Non-negotiable rules (Andon, just entry point, batch operations)
LSP-first navigation for .rs files (forbidden vs. required)
Chicago TDD (no mocks, real collaborators, AAA pattern)
OTEL span verification for LLM/external services
C4 model, real sync flow, error map, 54 stub classification
Workspace health assessment (54 stubs, 8,900 dead lines, 4 P0 blockers)
Common errors, root causes, fixes (timeout, flaky tests, build failures)
New developer onboarding (<5 min to first success)

front_matter_schema.auto_load: boolean — auto-load on session start (true for _core/*)
front_matter_schema.category: string — topic (rust, rdf, andon, testing, otel, etc.)
front_matter_schema.version: string — semantic version for rule version tracking
front_matter_schema.deprecated: boolean — mark rule as no longer in use

examples: Developer opens CLAUDE.md → sees all 16 commands, workflow, architecture in one place
Developer hovers over rustfmt error → docs/troubleshooting.md#fmt-check linked in error message
Developer asks 'how do I run tests?' → `just --list` shows test-unit, test, test-doc with one-liner descriptions
New developer → docs/QUICK_START.md → rustup + just + cargo watch → `just test-unit` passes in <5 min

dimensions.name: Hot Reload / Iteration
dimensions.description: Fast feedback loops that let developers verify changes in <2 seconds.
dimensions.why_it_matters: Feedback delay kills productivity. A <2s edit-save-test loop keeps flow intact. A >10s loop breaks concentration and kills iteration speed. Every 10ms saved on feedback × 100 edits/day = 16 minutes saved per dev per day.
success_criteria: `just check` completes in <5s (workspace check, no build)
`just test-unit` completes in <10s (unit tests only, no integration/doc tests)
Hot reload works: `cargo watch -x 'just test-unit'` triggers on file save
Incremental compilation <2s (Cargo dependency caching optimized)
First build <15s (SLO target in .claude/settings.json)
Doc examples validated without full workspace rebuild
Test failure feedback includes exact line and assertion
Compiler errors formatted with actionable context (not raw diagnostic)

fast_feedback_loops.verifies: Examples compile and produce correct output

cargo_watch_setup.what_happens: On any .rs file save → cargo watch detects → runs `just test-unit` → 3-10s feedback

examples: Developer edits src/lib.rs → saves → cargo watch triggers `just test-unit` → 8s later: all unit tests green
Developer adds docstring example → saves → rust-analyzer highlights syntax error → fixes → saves → `just test-doc` validates in 45s
Developer edits test → saves → 2s feedback: test fails → identifies assertion mismatch → fixes → saves → 2s feedback: test passes

dimensions.name: Defaults
dimensions.description: Sensible project-wide defaults that reduce decisions and configuration burden.
dimensions.why_it_matters: Defaults are the path of least resistance. Good defaults (type-first, Chicago TDD, Andon protocol) make the right thing easy. Bad defaults (unwrap(), print! debugging, ignoring warnings) make the wrong thing easy. Defaults scale: 1 bad default affects 100+ developers.
success_criteria: Rust edition pinned (2021) and enforced (Cargo.toml, rust-toolchain.toml)
RUST_VERSION pinned (1.91.1) in .claude/settings.json
RNG_SEED fixed (42) for determinism in tests
RUST_BACKTRACE enabled (1) for better error messages
Chicago TDD assumed (no mocks by default; tests use real collaborators)
Type-first design default (encode invariants in types, not assertions)
OTEL tracing enabled (toggled via RUST_LOG env var, not code)
Error handling: Result<T,E> required (no unwrap/expect in production)
Logging uses structured log! macros (not print!/println!)
Formatting auto-applied (just fmt before commit)
SLO-first: performance targets set before implementation

code_defaults.enforced_by: Test code review, lint rules, testing-forbidden.md
code_defaults.exception: Legitimate test infrastructure in tests/smoke/ and tests-archive/
code_defaults.impact: Tests prove real behavior, not mock interactions

code_defaults.example: PackageId newtype, typestate for Draft/Published, PhantomData for state machines

code_defaults.impact: Graceful error handling, better error messages

code_defaults.default: Logging via log! macros, not print!/println!
code_defaults.reason: Structured logging, log levels, filters (not possible with print!)

code_defaults.default: RDF is source of truth (edit .ttl, not .md)
code_defaults.impact: Single source of truth, no hand-crafted docs drift

code_defaults.example: Random number generators seeded, order-dependent tests fail consistently

code_defaults.impact: Consistent code style, no style debates
LLM/external service calls visible without code changes

code_defaults.example: GGEN-TPL-001: template variable not in SELECT
code_defaults.impact: Developers can search docs for specific errors

examples: New developer: 'How do I structure a test?' → Chicago TDD default → no mocks, real collaborators, AAA pattern
Developer writes `let x = load().unwrap();` → clippy flags it → replaces with `Result<_,_>` handling
Developer edits generated .md file → ggen sync overwrites it → lesson: edit .ttl, not .md
LLM call not working? → `export RUST_LOG=trace,ggen_ai=trace && cargo test` → OTEL spans visible without code change

command_ergonomics: ✓ All 15+ critical commands defined in justfile
✓ Timeout wrappers on all long-running commands
✓ No direct cargo/cargo make calls required
error_messages: ✓ Compiler errors include file:line and context
✓ GGEN-* diagnostic codes linked to recovery paths
✓ Error messages name the rule violated
ide_integration: ✓ Hover shows type signatures, trait bounds
✓ Go to Definition, Find References, Find Implementations all working
documentation_surface: ✓ CLAUDE.md is global source of truth
defaults: ✓ Result<T,E> over unwrap (zero unwrap in production)
✓ OTEL tracing toggleable via env var

definition_of_done_gates.requirement: All critical tasks callable via `just <task>` with <specified timeout>
definition_of_done_gates.verification: `just --list` shows ≥15 tasks; `just <task>` runs with correct timeout

definition_of_done_gates.requirement: All compiler/validation errors include file:line, rule violated, and recovery path
definition_of_done_gates.verification: New error messages reviewed for actionability; GGEN-* codes referenced

definition_of_done_gates.requirement: Common dev tasks take <5s or have cargo watch integration
rust-analyzer-lsp works; LSP navigation enforced for .rs files
definition_of_done_gates.verification: LSP workspaceSymbol, goToDefinition, findReferences all functional
New developers can complete QUICK_START in <5 min; error messages link to docs

definition_of_done_gates.requirement: Chicago TDD, type-first, Result<T,E>, OTEL tracing all default
definition_of_done_gates.verification: Code review enforces defaults; RUST_LOG and .claude/settings.json match

notes.golden_rule: Developer experience is complete when the path of least resistance is the right path. Defaults should encode best practices, not require discipline.
notes.evolution: This Definition of Done should be reviewed and updated quarterly. As pain points emerge in daily development, add them to a backlog and prioritize in the next sprint.
notes.measurement: DX success metrics: (1) time-to-first-success for new devs (<5 min), (2) time-to-resolution for common errors (<2 min), (3) feedback loop latency (<2s), (4) defect escape rate (goal: 0 pre-commit violations reaching main).
